headline: NBC Doubles Down on P.I. Revival with Boreanaz 'Rockford' Reboot
lede: Network programming executives are betting big on nostalgic procedural formats, ordering four high-profile series that blend classic IP with contemporary storytelling approaches.
body:
NBC is signaling a strategic pivot back to character-driven detective narratives with its latest development slate, anchoring the 2026-27 season with an ambitious slate of private investigator-themed programming.

David Boreanaz will headline the network's marquee revival of the iconic 1970s series *The Rockford Files*, modernizing the gritty private detective format that originally starred James Garner. Industry insiders suggest the reboot will maintain the original's sardonic humor while updating technological investigative methods.

Companioning the *Rockford Files* revival, Jake Johnson will lead a comedic take on the private detective genre, promising a lighter counterpoint to Boreanaz's presumably more dramatic approach. The comedy aims to deconstruct traditional P.I. tropes with Johnson's signature dry comedic sensibility.

Additional orders include Peter Krause's *Line of Fire* and *Newlyweds*, starring Téa Leoni and Tim Daly, rounding out a slate that suggests NBC is prioritizing recognizable talent and nostalgic intellectual property.

The network's programming strategy reflects an industrywide trend of reimagining classic formats for contemporary audiences, leveraging established fan bases while introducing properties to younger viewers.
key_facts: Four new series ordered for 2026-27 season
Jake Johnson developing comedic P.I. series
Peter Krause and Téa Leoni also starring in new shows
